description: Docker Dev Environment - Fully containerized dev stack with Next.js, tRPC API, Supabase, and Redis via a single docker compose up command

userStories.title: Create base Dockerfile for the monorepo
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Dockerfile that installs Node.js 22, pnpm, and the Supabase CLI so the monorepo can build and run inside a container.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile at repo root uses node:22 base image
pnpm is installed via `corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate`
Supabase CLI is installed (via the official npm package `supabase` or direct binary download)
`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` runs successfully during image build using the workspace lockfile
Docker socket passthrough is documented in a comment (host /var/run/docker.sock needed for supabase service)
Image builds without errors: `docker build -t op-dev .`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create .env.docker with dev-safe defaults
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a checked-in environment file with all required env vars pre-filled with safe local dev defaults so I can start the stack with zero manual configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: `.env.docker` exists at the repo root and is committed to git (not in .gitignore)
File contains NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET using well-known Supabase local dev defaults
File contains REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
File contains APP_PORT=3100 as default
A `.env.docker.example` with inline comments explaining each variable is also created
No real production secrets appear in either file
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create docker-compose.yml with all services, hot-reload, and configurable port
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single `docker compose up` to start app, api, supabase, and redis with hot-reload via volume mounts and the app port configurable via APP_PORT.
acceptanceCriteria: `docker-compose.yml` exists at repo root and defines four services: `app`, `api`, `supabase`, `redis`
`app` service uses the repo Dockerfile, runs `pnpm w:app dev`, and exposes host port `${APP_PORT:-3100}` mapped to container port 3100
`api` service uses the repo Dockerfile, runs `pnpm w:api dev`, and exposes host port 3300 mapped to container port 3300

`supabase` service uses the repo Dockerfile, mounts `/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock`, runs `supabase start --config supabase/supabase-dev.toml`, and exposes ports 54321 (API), 54322 (DB), 54323 (Studio), 54324 (Inbucket)
`redis` service uses `redis:7-alpine` image and exposes port 6379
All services share a custom Docker network named `op-dev`
`app` and `api` services bind-mount the repo root (`.:/app`) for hot-reload
Named volumes `app_node_modules` and `api_node_modules` are mounted at `/app/node_modules` in their respective services to prevent the bind mount from overwriting installed packages
Named volumes `app_next` and `api_next` are mounted at `/app/apps/app/.next` and `/app/apps/api/.next` for Next.js build cache
`docker-compose.yml` references `env_file: .env.docker`
Running `APP_PORT=4000 docker compose up` exposes the app on localhost:4000
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify Supabase startup and database seeding inside Docker
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the containerized Supabase instance to start with the correct config and apply seed data so the app is immediately usable after `docker compose up`.
acceptanceCriteria: `supabase start` inside the `supabase` container runs against `supabase/supabase-dev.toml` without errors
Supabase Studio is accessible at http://localhost:54323 after `docker compose up`
Seed SQL from `supabase/seed.sql` is applied on first start (verify a seeded table exists via Studio or psql)
The `app` container can reach Supabase using NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL from `.env.docker` (no connection refused errors in app logs)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write Docker setup documentation
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want clear step-by-step instructions for the Docker dev environment so I can get started with only Docker Desktop installed.
acceptanceCriteria: A `docker/README.md` file is created with a Docker Dev Environment section
Documents prerequisites: Docker Desktop (macOS/Linux), no other local tools needed
Shows the single startup command: `docker compose up`
Documents how to change the port: `APP_PORT=4000 docker compose up`
Documents how to access the app (http://localhost:3100 by default), API (http://localhost:3300), and Supabase Studio (http://localhost:54323)
Documents teardown: `docker compose down` (keeps data) and `docker compose down -v` (wipes volumes/database)
Typecheck passes
